title: A method for verifiable open-source agent research
abstract: A deterministic demo dossier for verifiable open-source agent research.
technicalField: Autonomous research agents, evidence kernels, and reproducible open-source workflows.
problem: Agent research artifacts are difficult to trust when logs, verification, and publication review are disconnected.
background: Existing agent workflows often generate code or notes without a defensive-publication dossier and final publication gates.
proposedSolution: Create an Open Invention mission that records evidence, generates a prototype, runs tests, and publishes only after Sovryn gates pass.
architecture: Sovryn Controller, Node Alpha workspace, dossier generator, verification evidence, publication policy, and GitHub publisher.
algorithm: Accept a brief, generate the dossier structure, build a prototype scaffold, run verification, scan for secrets and unsafe content, and stage a GitHub release.
implementationNotes: This demo is static. The CLI generates equivalent artifacts under .sovryn/inventions/<slug>/.
priorArt: Manual or agent-assisted public research required.

priorArtMatrix.title: Manual GitHub search required for A method for verifiable open-source agent research
priorArtMatrix.overlap: Potential overlap unknown until a public-source adapter retrieves concrete results.
priorArtMatrix.difference: Difference analysis pending. This deterministic placeholder prevents unsupported novelty claims.

noveltyNotes: Treat defensive publication artifacts as first-class outputs of an autonomous research pipeline.
safetyNotes: Do not publish harmful artifacts or leaked secrets.
